jokes.text: Buenos dias, erin. Buenos dias, miguel.
jokes.setup: Michael insists on Spanish-only communication
jokes.punchline: Calls himself 'miguel' in forced Spanish practice
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael Scott overcommitment to a bit, giving himself a Spanish name shows his typical self-importance

jokes.text: I believe that every man, woman, and child in this country should learn how to speak spanish. They are our neighbors to the south, and to cancun.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his noble reason for learning Spanish
jokes.punchline: Reduces it to wanting to vacation in Cancun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael deflation - starts with high-minded cultural awareness, ends with selfish vacation motivation

jokes.text: Guten tag, herr michael. I don't understand why michael is wasting his time with spanish. I have it on very good authority that within 20 years, everyone will be speaking german. Or a chinese-german hybrid.
jokes.setup: Dwight responds to Michael's Spanish in German
jokes.punchline: Claims everyone will speak German or Chinese-German hybrid
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight - his Germanic obsession and bizarre future predictions perfectly in character

jokes.text: So I told him to mark everything with the international symbol for gender. And, um... I should have been more specific. Your office is full of genitalia.
jokes.setup: Jim gave Michael advice about marking gender
jokes.punchline: Michael literally drew genitalia on everything
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Jim's deadpan delivery of the absurd visual outcome of his well-intentioned but poorly explained advice

jokes.text: Eso es lo que dice el. 'that's what he says'? Damn it.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to say 'that's what she said' in Spanish
jokes.punchline: Realizes he said 'that's what he said' instead
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on Michael's signature catchphrase - his Spanish incompetence ruins his own favorite joke

jokes.text: Yo soy cancun.
jokes.setup: Michael continues attempting Spanish
jokes.punchline: Says 'I am Cancun' instead of proper Spanish
jokes.explanation: Michael's Spanish incompetence continues - absurd but fits the pattern

jokes.text: Like, 'I just delivered a baby. They didn't offer me a guarantee.'
jokes.setup: Michael suggests a delivery guarantee joke
jokes.punchline: Baby delivery vs package delivery wordplay
jokes.explanation: Generic dad joke level humor - predictable wordplay without cleverness

jokes.text: Can you smell the power of halperts? Power couple.
jokes.setup: Michael comments on Jim and Pam as sales team
jokes.punchline: Awkward 'smell the power' phrasing
jokes.explanation: Michael's awkward phrasing creates mild cringe - decent character moment

jokes.text: If you want people to put the best face on something, why would you get two people who probably have never cut the face off of anything in their lives?
jokes.setup: Michael says Jim and Pam will put the best face on the company
jokes.punchline: Dwight takes 'put a face on' literally and disturbingly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - takes innocent idiom to dark, literal extreme while maintaining complete seriousness

jokes.text: I don't even know who you are anymore.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests they work well together
jokes.punchline: Pam whispers dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Pam's mock-dramatic response to Jim's corporate speak

jokes.text: Did somebody order a hooker over here?
jokes.setup: Attractive woman arrives at office
jokes.punchline: Packer's inappropriate comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Packer crudeness - in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: You look exactly alike. Oh, no, we're actually married. We're not brother and sister.
jokes.setup: Donna says Jim and Pam look exactly alike
jokes.punchline: Pam clarifies they're married, not siblings
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward moment - guest character creates uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: I have a sense about these things. All right. You have some ancestors in common... somewhere back. I knew it. You should see their baby.
jokes.setup: Michael doubles down on the sibling theory
jokes.punchline: Implies their baby looks inbred
jokes.explanation: Michael making things worse - characteristic inability to let things go

jokes.text: It doesn't just sound a'ight, it sounds amazing. I didn't say 'a'ight.'
jokes.setup: Michael describes minority executive program to Darryl
jokes.punchline: Michael unconsciously code-switches, Darryl calls it out
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation of Michael's unconscious code-switching and immediate denial - good social comedy

jokes.text: Uh, glasses wearers. Cholera survivors. Geniuses. Non-organic family farmers. The list goes on and on.
jokes.setup: Michael lists minorities eligible for program
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous categories like cholera survivors and farmers
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - Michael's bizarre interpretation of minority categories

jokes.text: Ten seconds ago, this guy was driving a forklift. Okay? Now, all of a sudden, he's cinderella of the office.
jokes.setup: Dwight observes Darryl's sudden popularity
jokes.punchline: Calls him 'cinderella of the office'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight metaphor - unexpected fairy tale reference for workplace dynamics

jokes.text: You know what? You can go to hell. All right? Thanks for nothing. Oh, man. White people, right?
jokes.setup: Kelly has phone argument about wrong size order
jokes.punchline: Assumes caller is white, makes racial generalization
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kelly's assumptions and casual stereotyping fits her personality

jokes.text: I don't know if she was white. Well, you can kind of tell from the voice.
jokes.setup: Darryl questions Kelly's assumption about caller's race
jokes.punchline: Kelly doubles down on voice-based racial profiling
jokes.explanation: Continues the racial profiling joke with Kelly's confident assertion

jokes.text: How many indian ceos can you think of? I can't think of any ceos, any race.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about Indian CEO role models
jokes.punchline: Kelly admits she can't think of any CEOs at all
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of Michael's attempt at inspiration - Kelly's honesty undercuts the whole premise

jokes.text: I could be the indian julia roberts. That's not-- she's--okay.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests Kelly could be like Indian Bill Gates
jokes.punchline: Kelly suggests Indian Julia Roberts, confusing the concept
jokes.explanation: Good Kelly moment - completely misunderstands business inspiration for Hollywood glamour

jokes.text: It's subtle. That's how it works. I show her an image that turns her on. And then she looks at me, then she looks at the image, then back at me, then back at the image. Soon, she doesn't know what is me, what is the image. She just knows that she's turned on.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his seduction strategy with photos
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous psychological manipulation theory
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion - elaborate psychological theory for obvious behavior, delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Who took the photo? / Ryan.
jokes.setup: Michael showing romantic solo photos
jokes.punchline: Revealing another man took the intimate photos
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward reveal - undermines Michael's romantic gesture completely

jokes.text: You didn't see it, pam. She was giving me all sorts of signals that she wanted me to make a move.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his interpretation of Donna's behavior
jokes.punchline: His complete misreading of professional politeness
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael delusion - misreading social cues fits his character but not particularly original

jokes.text: Oh, really, jim? What about pam? And you did a heck of a lot more than kiss. Maybe this could be my pam.
jokes.setup: Jim criticizes Michael for kissing at work
jokes.punchline: Michael references Jim and Pam's office relationship
jokes.explanation: Decent deflection by Michael - uses Jim's own history against him

jokes.text: But I work in the nightlife industry. I get hit on all the time. In my 20s, it would have been annoying. In my late 20s, I find it really flattering.
jokes.setup: Donna explains her reaction to Michael's advance
jokes.punchline: Age-based perspective shift on harassment
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about aging and attention - realistic character perspective

jokes.text: Don't act like you understood anything that guy said.
jokes.setup: Jim mentions the Schwab guy's advice
jokes.punchline: Pam calls out Jim's bluff about understanding
jokes.explanation: Good marital humor - Pam cutting through Jim's corporate posturing

jokes.text: Indian guys always wear their cell phones outside their pants. It's so dorky. Oh, no, no. That's not dorky. Look, it's easily accessible. Boom, like this. 911, hello. Scranton strangler's in the house. Inside the house.
jokes.setup: Kelly complains about Indian men wearing phones externally
jokes.punchline: Michael demonstrates with Scranton Strangler emergency
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Michael's demonstration accidentally becomes a callback to the show's running serial killer storyline

jokes.text: You ever notice you can only ooze two things: sexuality and pus.
jokes.setup: Discussion about someone oozing sexuality
jokes.punchline: Kevin's crude biological observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin moment - unexpectedly insightful observation delivered with characteristic bluntness. Perfect economy and quotable.

jokes.text: Maybe she's just pushing her breasts together to make them look bigger. Like that?
jokes.setup: Kevin suggests woman is making breasts look bigger
jokes.punchline: Angela demonstrates the gesture
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Angela participating in crude conversation is against character type

jokes.text: Actually, I ran the numbers on this, and in this case, it makes more financial sense to gain money.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests losing money for tax purposes
jokes.punchline: Oscar's matter-of-fact rejection of nonsensical logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar deadpan - states the obvious with accounting precision, deflating Jim's nonsensical sales pitch

jokes.text: 'cause I stopped by your desk like 15 times a day. I was after your money. Well, the joke was on you.
jokes.setup: Pam admits she was flirting for Jim's money
jokes.punchline: Jim points out he had no money
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship humor - self-deprecating joke about their courtship

jokes.text: This girl was really rude to me at the mall. So I created a fake I.M. Account from a hot guy at her high school, and now I'm trying to make her anorexic. Tell her everyone in home room thinks she's fat.
jokes.setup: Ryan explains his computer activity
jokes.punchline: Elaborate catfishing scheme to cause eating disorder
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny Ryan moment - his casual sociopathy perfectly fits his character development

jokes.text: When I become executive, I'm gonna make Ryan manager. And then the two of us are cleaning house.
jokes.setup: Kelly getting excited about potential promotion
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning nepotism and office purge
jokes.explanation: Classic Kelly - immediately corrupted by tiny amount of hypothetical power

jokes.text: Do you think you're treated differently because of your race? Would you ask that same question if I was white? We're so in.
jokes.setup: Kelly practices interview deflection
jokes.punchline: Assumes they've already won with the tactic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kelly's overconfidence in Ryan's coaching

jokes.text: If you'd have told me this morning that today I'd be creating a monster capable of my own destruction, I'd have thought you were referring to the bull moose Mose and I are trying to reanimate.
jokes.setup: Dwight realizes Kelly will be a threat
jokes.punchline: References reanimating a bull moose with Mose
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Dwight line - brilliant structure building to absurd Schrute farm activities. Perfect character integration and quotable absurdity.

jokes.text: It's like cool... 'certified cool.' I like how they say 'certified.' Like there's some consortium of...
jokes.setup: Jim comments on mint packaging
jokes.punchline: Trails off about certification consortium
jokes.explanation: Decent Jim observation - noting the absurdity of marketing language

jokes.text: Well, why don't you just move the m&ms? Well, why don't you shut up!
jokes.setup: Kevin compares Michael's situation to being tempted by M&Ms
jokes.punchline: Kevin's hostile reaction to logical solution
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - his angry response to having his analogy questioned

jokes.text: What sort of movie would rudy have been if he had just stopped, given up, after two rejections? Would have been a lot shorter. Probably been a lot funnier.
jokes.setup: Michael references Rudy as inspiration
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan improvement suggestions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim deadpan - deflating Michael's inspirational movie reference with practical film criticism

jokes.text: Indians migrated from the caucuses region of europe. Therefore, technically, she is caucasian. You're welcome, america.
jokes.setup: Dwight tries to disqualify Kelly from minority program
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific racial classification ending with patriotic flourish
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - pseudo-intellectual argument with grandiose patriotic conclusion

jokes.text: I got my whole life to be a minority executive. I only have about a year left in these knees, though.
jokes.setup: Darryl explains withdrawing from executive program
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes softball over career advancement due to knee health
jokes.explanation: Excellent Darryl line - perfectly captures his character's priorities and practical wisdom with great economy

jokes.text: I do find that offensive, actually.
jokes.setup: Michael asks about meaning of her necklace
jokes.punchline: Donna's blunt response to his question
jokes.explanation: Straightforward awkward moment - guest character shutting down Michael's inappropriate question

jokes.text: Do you need validation? We don't-- we don't validate.
jokes.setup: Michael offers validation (parking)
jokes.punchline: Realizes they don't offer parking validation
jokes.explanation: Minor Michael moment - another example of his poor planning and execution

jokes.text: And have a good elevator ride.
jokes.setup: Awkward goodbye conversation continues
jokes.punchline: Jim's desperate attempt at normal farewell
jokes.explanation: Decent cringe moment - Jim's social awkwardness in extended goodbye

jokes.text: I wasn't talking to you, pale face.
jokes.setup: Erin responds excitedly to Dwight's offer
jokes.punchline: Dwight's casual racial dismissal
jokes.explanation: Standard Dwight insensitivity - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'll slap you in the face with a rainbow.
jokes.setup: Discussion about diversity rainbow metaphor
jokes.punchline: Dwight's violent twist on inspirational imagery
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight line - takes corporate diversity speak and makes it absurdly threatening

jokes.text: For hobbies, um, yoga, belly dancing, snake charming. Beds of nails, I like lying on them.
jokes.setup: Kelly lists her hobbies for application
jokes.punchline: Escalates to stereotypical and absurd activities
jokes.explanation: Kelly playing up stereotypes - fits her character but relies on obvious cultural assumptions

jokes.text: This is Hide Toshi Hasagawa. He would like to apply for the sabre minority executive training program 'print in all colors' initiative.
jokes.setup: Dwight introduces someone to compete with Kelly
jokes.punchline: Elaborate formal introduction of random person
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight scheme - his formal presentation of his sabotage plan

jokes.text: God, I hate you so much! Caucasians, am I right?
jokes.setup: Kelly realizes Dwight's sabotage
jokes.punchline: Appeals to Hide based on shared minority status
jokes.explanation: Good Kelly moment - trying to create alliance against Dwight

jokes.text: In japan, heart surgeon, number one. Steady hand.
jokes.setup: Hide introduces himself for the program
jokes.punchline: References his background as top surgeon
jokes.explanation: Good callback - references Hide's previous character development and prestigious background

jokes.text: Was it the cleavage? Yeah, and the shoulder cutouts.
jokes.setup: Michael and Kevin analyze why they misread Donna's signals
jokes.punchline: Kevin specifically notes the 'shoulder cutouts' as a distraction factor
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin fashion awareness - unexpectedly specific about clothing details that distracted him

jokes.text: You know, when I tore my scrote, I was, uh... I was seeing this really hot urologist about it, and thought she was into me. But now I think she was just doing a bunch of stuff to bill my hmo.
jokes.setup: Creed relates to Michael's misreading of professional attention
jokes.punchline: Graphic personal medical story with billing revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - inappropriate oversharing with medical details and naive realization about billing

jokes.text: They're going to be pretty pleased in tallahassee that I snagged an indian for the program. She'll be the first. The program's mostly black. It's almost too black. That didn't sound right.
jokes.setup: Michael celebrates getting Kelly into the program
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate racial commentary
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael racial insensitivity - his attempt at self-correction makes it worse

jokes.text: The program's mostly black. It's almost too black.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the diversity program demographics
jokes.punchline: His awkward racial commentary
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael racial awkwardness but lacks the clever specificity of his best moments

jokes.text: Just once, I would like to be a puppet master and have nothing go wrong. Is that too much to ask?
jokes.setup: Dwight's plan backfired with Kelly getting the position
jokes.punchline: His frustrated plea about his manipulation schemes
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - his frustrated admission of his constant scheming and failure

jokes.text: You were right. / About what? / You were right. / I'm sorry. / No, no. No. / I'm sor-- / No.
jokes.setup: Michael and Donna's final awkward encounter
jokes.punchline: The painful back-and-forth of mutual misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good cringe ending that captures the painful awkwardness of mismatched expectations

jokes.text: You were right. About what? You were right.
jokes.setup: Michael returns her barrette saying everyone was right about her disinterest
jokes.punchline: Donna reveals he was actually right about her interest
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist - subverts expectations by revealing Michael was actually correct about the signals
